---
name: edit-discipline
description: Use when implementing or modifying code, configuration, scripts, or documentation.
---

# Edit Discipline

- Understand surrounding conventions before editing.
- Prefer the smallest coherent change that solves the request.
- Reuse existing files, helpers, and abstractions before creating new ones.
- Use subagents only for targeted research or validation; do not offload ownership of the implementation.
- Run the narrowest meaningful validation after significant changes.
- If a change introduces new commands, configuration, or workflow expectations, update the local docs or prompts in the same task.
- Be explicit about what was verified, what was inferred, and what remains unverified.
